audience: Business owners, employees, HR teams and marketing leads planning professional portraits for one person or a complete team
primaryDutchSearchIntent: Professionals en KMO's helpen een passende portretstijl, voorbereiding en consistente beeldselectie te kiezen voor website, LinkedIn, pers en teamcommunicatie

searchIntent.question: How do you prepare professional corporate portraits that look natural, suit each person's role and remain consistent across a team?
searchIntent.geographicContext: English-speaking professionals and SMEs in Limburg, Belgium and the wider Belgian market

longTailKeywords: what to wear for a corporate headshot
portrait photos for website and LinkedIn
directAnswer: A strong corporate portrait should suit the person, their role and the organisation. Start with where the image will be used, then align expression, clothing, location, lighting, pose, crop and editing. For a team, keep the photographic system consistent without forcing every person into the same pose or expression.
title: Corporate portraits: 12 practical tips for stronger team photos
metaDescription: Planning corporate portraits? Choose the right style, clothing, location, lighting and poses, then build a consistent set for your website and LinkedIn.
h1: Corporate portraits: practical tips for stronger team photos
factsToPreserve: The best portrait is not automatically the most formal; style should follow the person, role, organisation and intended channel
Team consistency comes mainly from lighting, framing, background family, colour treatment and selection criteria, not identical poses or expressions
A wider master image enables square, vertical and horizontal crops for different channels
LinkedIn profile photographs must represent the person's likeness and should not be replaced by a standalone logo, landscape or stock image
Alt text depends on the image's function and should not repeat adjacent information unnecessarily or contain keyword stuffing
High-resolution print masters should not be loaded directly into every web component
Consent to create an image and consent to publish or distribute it are separate issues
Copyright, file delivery and a usage licence are not automatically the same
The legal section is practical guidance and not legal advice
Retouching should remove temporary distractions without changing permanent features or body shape until the person is no longer recognisable
No portrait, expression or image alone guarantees a click, application or enquiry
Growing teams should document the photographic setup so new employees can be added consistently
